calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Sanctions Against David C. Simmons
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Willam Taylor for Marquez Simmons (moving party). Petitioner in Propria Persona David Simmons is present.

Mr. Marquez stated that he submits to the tenative ruling and requested to strike the request for sanctions against David Simmons. The Court granted his request.

The Court adopts the tenative ruling as modified: Petitioner's Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is granted (order signed in open court).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: N. Wong/ J. Alameda. Court Attorney: Elif Sonmez-Kirsch. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. Matter Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-97-269206 Estate of Roscoe Simmons & PTR-15-298700 Simmons Family Trust
Appearances: Evan Cote (via court call) for (petitioner) David Simmons.

The Court stated that it needs courtesy copies for both cases. Mr. Cote stated that courtesy copies were personally delivered on 12/18/15. The Court requested that additional copies be delivered and took the motions to be relieved as counsel (in both case numbers) under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evan Cote (via court call) for David Simmons who is present in court. Mark Simmons in Propria Persona. Joyce Simmons also present.

Mr. Cote stated that the probate examiner says that this should be transferred to the civil department, that they are seeking appointment of an administrator, that assets will be transferred to the trust and that it is not proper to preclude his client from contesting. Mark Simmons reported that he is trying to obtain counsel, that it is a financial burden and that he needs a 3 week continuance to get someone in place. David Simmons stated that he would like to move on this and that he will follow his attorney's advice. Mr. Cote stated that at the last hearing Mark Simmons requested a continuance to obtain counsel and that he is unsure if a further continuance will accomplish that. The Court stated that it will not grant a continuance and took the petition for letters of administration under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-03-2015 Continued To Sep-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evan Cote (by Court Call) for Petitioners David Simmons and Joyce Simmons (both present in court); Mark Anthony Simmons
Mr. Cote advised the Court that he had only recently learned about the pour-over trust, and that there were elder abuse issues which could invalidate the trust and result in the trust assets being returned to the estate. Mark Simmons informed the Court that he was seeking counsel to represent him in the matter, and requested that the Court continue the matter for thirty days. The Court asked Mr. Cote for his response to the request for a continuance of the hearing. Mr. Cote replied that it would not be inappropriate for the continuance to be granted to enable Mark Simmons to find counsel to represent him, but that his clients should be granted special letters as temporary administrators of the estate to enable them to file an action for elder abuse. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to September 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Cote will provide the Court with a proposed order regarding his requested powers.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
